requirements.section_title: CONNECT – Client requests a connection to a Server
requirements.summary: After a Network Connection is established by a Client to a Server, the first Packet sent from the Client to the Server MUST be a CONNECT Packet .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.section_title: CONNECT – Client requests a connection to a Server
requirements.summary: A Client can only send the CONNECT Packet once over a Network Connection. The Server MUST process a second CONNECT Packet sent from a Client as a protocol violation and disconnect the Client . See section 4.8 for information about handling errors.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the protocol name is incorrect the Server MAY disconnect the Client, or it MAY continue processing the CONNECT packet in accordance with some other specification. In the latter case, the Server MUST NOT continue to process the CONNECT packet in line with this specification .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: The 8 bit unsigned value that represents the revision level of the protocol used by the Client. The value of the Protocol Level field for the version 3.1.1 of the protocol is 4 (0x04). The Server MUST respond to the CONNECT Packet with a CONNACK return code 0x01 (unacceptable...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: The Server MUST validate that the reserved flag in the CONNECT Control Packet is set to zero and disconnect the Client if it is not zero .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If CleanSession is set to 0, the Server MUST resume communications with the Client based on state from the current Session (as identified by the Client identifier). If there is no Session associated with the Client identifier the Server MUST create a new Session.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If CleanSession is set to 0, the Server MUST resume communications with the Client based on state from the current Session (as identified by the Client identifier). If there is no Session associated with the Client identifier the Server MUST create a new Session.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If CleanSession is set to 1, the Client and Server MUST discard any previous Session and start a new one. This Session lasts as long as the Network Connection. State data associated with this Session MUST NOT be reused in any subsequent Session .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 1 this indicates that, if the Connect request is accepted, a Will Message MUST be stored on the Server and associated with the Network Connection. The Will Message MUST be published when the Network Connection is subsequently closed unless the Will M...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will QoS and Will Retain fields in the Connect Flags will be used by the Server, and the Will Topic and Will Message fields MUST be present in the payload .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: The Will Message MUST be removed from the stored Session state in the Server once it has been published or the Server has received a DISCONNECT packet from the Client .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 0 the Will QoS and Will Retain fields in the Connect Flags MUST be set to zero and the Will Topic and Will Message fields MUST NOT be present in the payload .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 0, a Will Message MUST NOT be published when this Network Connection ends .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will QoS MUST be set to 0 (0x00) .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the value of Will QoS can be 0 (0x00), 1 (0x01), or 2 (0x02). It MUST NOT be 3 (0x03) .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will Retain Flag MUST be set to 0 .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If Will Retain is set to 0, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a non-retained message .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If Will Retain is set to 1, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a retained message .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the User Name Flag is set to 0, a user name MUST NOT be present in the payload .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the User Name Flag is set to 1, a user name MUST be present in the payload .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Password Flag is set to 0, a password MUST NOT be present in the payload .

requirements.summary: If the Password Flag is set to 1, a password MUST be present in the payload .

requirements.summary: The Keep Alive is a time interval measured in seconds. Expressed as a 16-bit word, it is the maximum time interval that is permitted to elapse between the point at which the Client finishes transmitting one Control Packet and the point it starts sending the next.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: If the Keep Alive value is non-zero and the Server does not receive a Control Packet from the Client within one and a half times the Keep Alive time period, it MUST disconnect the Network Connection to the Client as if the network had failed .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping from section prefix 3.1 to packet module.

requirements.summary: When a Client reconnects with CleanSession set to 0, both the Client and Server MUST re-send any unacknowledged PUBLISH Packets (where QoS > 0) and PUBREL Packets using their original Packet Identifiers . This is the only circumstance where a Client or Server is REQUIRED to re...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When a Server takes ownership of an incoming Application Message it MUST add it to the Session state of those clients that have matching Subscriptions. Matching rules are defined in Section 4.7 .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Under normal circumstances Clients receive messages in response to Subscriptions they have created. A Client could also receive messages that do not match any of its explicit Subscriptions. This can happen if the Server automatically assigned a subscription to the Client.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When it re-sends any PUBLISH packets, it MUST re-send them in the order in which the original PUBLISH packets were sent (this applies to QoS 1 and QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: It MUST send PUBACK packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBLISH packets were received (QoS 1 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: It MUST send PUBREC packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBLISH packets were received (QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: It MUST send PUBREL packets in the order in which the corresponding PUBREC packets were received (QoS 2 messages)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: A Server MUST by default treat each Topic as an "Ordered Topic". It MAY provide an administrative or other mechanism to allow one or more Topics to be treated as an "Unordered Topic" .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When a Server processes a message that has been published to an Ordered Topic, it MUST follow the rules listed above when delivering messages to each of its subscribers. In addition it MUST send PUBLISH packets to consumers (for the same Topic and QoS) in the order that they w...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The wildcard characters can be used in Topic Filters, but MUST NOT be used within a Topic Name .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The number sign (‘#’ U+0023) is a wildcard character that matches any number of levels within a topic. The multi-level wildcard represents the parent and any number of child levels. The multi-level wildcard character MUST be specified either on its own or following a topic lev...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The single-level wildcard can be used at any level in the Topic Filter, including first and last levels. Where it is used it MUST occupy an entire level of the filter . It can be used at more than one level in the Topic Filter and can be used in conjunction with the multilevel...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: The Server MUST NOT match Topic Filters starting with a wildcard character (# or +) with Topic Names beginning with a $ character . The Server SHOULD prevent Clients from using such Topic Names to exchange messages with other Clients.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: All Topic Names and Topic Filters MUST be at least one character long
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Topic Names and Topic Filters MUST NOT include the null character (Unicode U+0000)
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Topic Names and Topic Filters are UTF-8 encoded strings, they MUST NOT encode to more than 65535 bytes . See Section 1.5.3
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: When it performs subscription matching the Server MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters . Each non-wildcarded level in the Topic Filter has to match the corresponding level in the Topi...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: Unless stated otherwise, if either the Server or Client encounters a protocol violation, it MUST close the Network Connection on which it received that Control Packet which caused the protocol violation .
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.summary: If the Client or Server encounters a Transient Error while processing an inbound Control Packet it MUST close the Network Connection on which it received that Control Packet . If a Server detects a Transient Error it SHOULD NOT disconnect or have any other effect on its intera...
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to state machine, event loop, and topic behavior modules.

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · MQTT Control Packets MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames. If any other type of data frame is received the recipient MUST close the Network Connection .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · A single WebSocket data frame can contain multiple or partial MQTT Control Packets. The receiver MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · The client MUST include “mqtt” in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols it offers .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.section_title: Using WebSocket as a network transport
requirements.summary: · The WebSocket Sub Protocol name selected and returned by the server MUST be “mqtt” .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 6 (Using WebSocket as a network transport).

requirements.summary: An MQTT implementation MAY conform as both an MQTT Client and MQTT Server implementation. A Server that both accepts inbound connections and establishes outbound connections to other Servers MUST conform as both an MQTT Client and MQTT Server .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 7 (Conformance).

requirements.summary: Conformant implementations MUST NOT require the use of any extensions defined outside of this specification in order to interoperate with any other conformant implementation .
requirements.mapping_reason: Fallback mapping for section 7 (Conformance).

requirements.summary: A conformant Server MUST support the use of one or more underlying transport protocols that provide an ordered, lossless, stream of bytes from the Client to Server and Server to Client . However conformance does not depend on it supporting any specific transport protocols.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to tests and protocol modules for conformance coverage.

requirements.summary: A conformant Client MUST support the use of one or more underlying transport protocols that provide an ordered, lossless, stream of bytes from the Client to Server and Server to Client . However conformance does not depend on it supporting any specific transport protocols.
requirements.mapping_reason: Heuristic mapping to tests and protocol modules for conformance coverage.
